description:
You are acting as the site administrator.  
A text file that lists the (very small) current user-account data already exists at:

/home/user/admin/user_accounts.txt

Your job is to verify the integrity of that file and to create a permanent record of the checksum calculation.

Required actions
1. Calculate the SHA-256 checksum of /home/user/admin/user_accounts.txt.
2. Create (or overwrite if it already exists) a log file named /home/user/admin/checksums.log that contains exactly one line with BOTH of the following fields separated by two blank spaces:
   • the full absolute path of the file that was hashed  
   • the 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256 digest

Output format that the automated test will look for (␠ indicates a literal space character):

/home/user/admin/user_accounts.txt␠␠<64-hex-chars>

Example (DO NOT copy these values):
/home/user/admin/user_accounts.txt  ffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff

Important rules
• The line in /home/user/admin/checksums.log must end with a single newline character “\n”.  
• Use exactly two space characters between the path and the checksum. No tabs or extra spaces.  
• No additional lines or text are allowed in the log file.  
• Do not alter /home/user/admin/user_accounts.txt in any way.

Success criteria
The task is complete when /home/user/admin/checksums.log exists and its single line follows the format shown above with the correct SHA-256 digest for the current contents of /home/user/admin/user_accounts.txt.
truth:
Initial filesystem state created by the grader
• Directory: /home/user/admin/  (permissions 755)
• File: /home/user/admin/user_accounts.txt  (permissions 644)
  Exact contents (4 bytes, no trailing newline):
  test

The correct SHA-256 digest of that exact 4-byte file is:
9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08

Grader verification logic
1. Confirm that /home/user/admin/checksums.log exists and is a regular file.
2. Read the single line of that file; reject if there is more than one line or if the line does not terminate with “\n”.
3. Split on two consecutive space characters. The left token must be the literal string “/home/user/admin/user_accounts.txt”.
4. The right token must equal the 64-character digest
   9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08
name: task_000000_72661da9
